2026 in Rizin Fighting Federation
Information
First dateMarch 7, 2026 (2026-03-07)
Events
Fights
Chronology
2025 in Rizin Fighting Federation 2026 in Rizin Fighting Federation

The year 2026 will be the 12th year in the history of the Rizin Fighting Federation, a mixed martial arts promotion based in Japan.

List of events

[edit]
# Event Date Venue Location
1 Rizin 52 Mar 7, 2026 Ariake Arena Tokyo, Japan
2 Rizin Landmark 13 Apr 12, 2026 Marine Messe Fukuoka Fukuoka, Japan
3 Rizin 53 May 10, 2026 Glion Arena Kobe Kobe, Japan
4 Rizin Landmark 14 Jun 6, 2026 Xebio Arena Sendai Sendai, Japan
5 Rizin Landmark 15 Jul 18, 2026 Hiroshima Prefectural Sports Center Hiroshima, Japan
6 Rizin 54 Aug 11, 2026 Toyota Arena Tokyo Kōtō, Japan
7 Super Rizin 5 Sep 10, 2026 Kyocera Dome Osaka Osaka, Japan

Rizin Japan Heavyweight Grand Prix

[edit]

Background

[edit]

On April 23, 2026, it was announced that the "RIZIN JAPAN GP Heavyweight Tournament" will take place in 2026, which semifinals matches will be held at Rizin 54 in August, and the two winners will advance to the final match scheduled in November event. The tournament taking part as participants: Mikio Ueda, Tsuyoshi Sudario, King Edokpolo, and opponent TBA fighter. The winner of the tournament should face Alexander Soldatkin on New Year's Eve event and compete for inaugural heavyweight title.[1]

Tournament bracket

[edit]
Semifinal Final
      
Mikio Ueda
Tsuyoshi Sudario
King Edokpolo

Rizin 52

[edit]
Rizin 52
PromotionRizin Fighting Federation
DateMarch 7, 2026
VenueAriake Arena
CityTokyo, Japan

Rizin 52 was a mixed martial arts event that was held by Rizin Fighting Federation on March 7, 2026, at the Ariake Arena in Tokyo, Japan.[2]

Background

[edit]

A featherweight bout between Kyoma Akimoto and former Bellator Bantamweight World Champion Patchy Mix headlined this event.[3][4]

Results

[edit]
Main card (Rizin.tv)
Weight Class Method Round T.Time Notes
Featherweight 66 kg Kyoma Akimoto def. Patchy Mix TKO (punches and soccer kicks) 2 0:37
Lightweight 71 kg Luiz Gustavo def. Taisei Sakuraba KO (punch) 2 2:32
Featherweight 66 kg Ryo Takagi def. Shuya Kimura TKO (doctor stoppage) 1 5:00
Featherweight 66 kg Koji Takeda def. Viktor Kolesnik Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
Intermission (Rizin.tv)
Super Atomweight 49 kg Saori Oshima def. Keito Oyama Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
Catchweight 59 kg Yuki Ito def. Carlos Mota KO (head kick and punches) 1 2:27
Bantamweight 61 kg Jinnosuke Kashimura def. Hideo Tokoro Technical Submission (anaconda choke) 1 1:06
Lightweight 71 kg Kim Kyung-pyo def. Yusuke Yachi TKO (doctor stoppage) 2 4:25
Bantamweight 61 kg Shoko Sato def. John Sweeney Submission (triangle choke) 1 4:49
Flyweight 57 kg Tony Laramie def. Takaki Soya TKO (punches) 3 2:00
Opening Ceremony (YouTube)
Flyweight 57 kg Lee Jung-hyun def. Jo Arai TKO (knees and punches) 3 3:44
Super Atomweight 49 kg Noeru Narita def. Lee Bo-mi Submission (guillotine choke) 1 2:08

Rizin Landmark 13

[edit]
Rizin Landmark 13 in Fukuoka
PromotionRizin Fighting Federation
DateApril 12, 2026
VenueMarine Messe Fukuoka
CityFukuoka, Japan

Rizin Landmark 13 in Fukuoka was a combat sports event that was held by Rizin Fighting Federation on April 12, 2026, at Marine Messe Fukuoka in Fukuoka, Japan.

Background

[edit]

A Rizin Featherweight Championship bout between current champion Razhabali Shaydullaev and former K-1 Welterweight Champion Yuta Kubo headlined the event.[5] The pairing previously fought at Rizin 49 in December 2024, which Shaydullaev won by second round TKO.[6]

A Rizin Bantamweight Championship bout between current champion Danny Sabatello and Joji Goto co-headlined the event.[7]

Results

[edit]
Main card (Rizin.tv)
Weight Class Method Round T.Time Notes
Featherweight 66 kg Razhabali Shaydullaev (c) def. Yuta Kubo TKO (punches) 1 4:13 For the Rizin Featherweight Championship.
Bantamweight 61 kg Danny Sabatello (c) def. Joji Goto Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00 For the Rizin Bantamweight Championship.
Lightweight 71 kg Yoshinori Horie def. Patricky Pitbull Decision (split) 3 5:00
Featherweight 66 kg Kyohei Hagiwara vs. Abijah Kaleo Meheula No Contest (missed weight) 1 4:49 Meheula missed weight (67.5 kg). Originally ruled a TKO (knees and punches) win for Meheula; overturned by promoter due to him missing weight.
Intermission (Rizin.tv)
Bantamweight 61 kg Azizbek Temirov def. Ryuya Fukuda KO (punch) 2 1:40
Featherweight 66 kg (Kickboxing) Taio Asahisa def. Singphayak HamaGym KO (punch to the body) 1 1:45
Flyweight 57 kg Makoto Takahashi def. Nkazimulo Zulu Submission (kimura) 1 2:52
Super Atomweight 49 kg Natalia Kuziutina def. Ayaka Hamasaki Submission (armbar) 1 4:54
Featherweight 66 kg Kazumasa Majima def. James Gallagher Technical Submission (arm-triangle choke) 3 2:34
Lightweight 71 kg Nurkhan Zhumagazy def. Tenya Yoshimura Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00 Yoshimura missed weight (74.5 kg).
Flyweight 57 kg Kyohei Takagi def. Ryosuke Honda TKO (punches) 1 4:59
Bantamweight 61 kg Hyuga Miyagawa def. Seiya Inoue Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Opening Ceremony: Rizin MMA Special Rules (5min / 2R)

Rizin 53

[edit]
Rizin 53
PromotionRizin Fighting Federation
DateMay 10, 2026
VenueGlion Arena Kobe
CityKobe, Japan

Rizin 53 is an upcoming combat sports event that will be held by Rizin Fighting Federation on May 10, 2026, at the Glion Arena Kobe in Kobe, Japan.

Background

[edit]

A Rizin Lightweight Championship bout between current champion Ilkhom Nozimov and Luiz Gustavo is scheduled to headline the event.[8]

[edit]

Rizin 54

[edit]
Rizin 54
PromotionRizin Fighting Federation
DateAugust 11, 2026
VenueToyota Arena Tokyo
CityKōtō, Japan

Rizin 54 is an upcoming combat sports event that will be held by Rizin Fighting Federation on August 11, 2026, at Toyota Arena Tokyo in Kōtō, Japan.

Background

[edit]

The semifinals of Rizin Japan Heavyweight Grand Prix are scheduled to take place at this event.[1]

Fight card

[edit]
Main card (Rizin.tv)
Weight Class Method Round T.Time Notes
Heavyweight 120 kg Mikio Ueda vs. Tsuyoshi Sudario Rizin Japan Heavyweight Grand Prix Semifinal.
Heavyweight 120 kg King Edokpolo vs. TBA Rizin Japan Heavyweight Grand Prix Semifinal.
